meta.setting: Back lots, kitchen doors, wash lines, ash pits, chicken runs, and small gardens behind French Gulch streets
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with domestic labor, rumor, scarcity, back-door deals, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, family and work settlement, roads, and lived regional context.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California town material, old buildings, commerce, and street/back-lot contrast.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Kitchen Lots region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Back Doors, Wash Lines, and Scrap Gardens
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: object, weather or track, plain meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing the lots as social pressure, resource, hidden route, and scene engine
closing blockquote that gives a generated back-lot sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate kitchen lots as lived-in backsides of town: gardens, wash lines, ash pits, gossip, chickens, dishwater, and hidden exchanges.
Make them social scenes where domestic labor meets secrets, scarcity, shelter, illness, and private bargaining.
Use onion skins, cracked basins, fence slats, ash barrels, bootprints, laundry, and side doors as field language.
The place should feel private but not small; whole-town politics pass through the back door.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Give this place onion skins, dishwater, and the kind of truth heard while nobody thinks they are in public. The back lot knows the town better than the boardwalk does.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A kitchen-lot scene should ask who saw the rider leave by the back door, who is hiding in the wash, and what hunger made negotiable.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western domestic and town-back language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/kitchen-lots.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make domestic labor quaint or decorative only
do not use modern plumbing, appliances, vehicles, plastic props, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about back-door social pressure, scarcity, hidden routes, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
